lyrics:
A candle deep

Some walk with fire hidden low
A glow within, a pulse they know
It's not for show, it doesn't blind
But lights the edges of the mind
Others pass with empty gaze
Lost in noise and shadowed days

They've never burned, so never yearn
For embers they can't discern

A candle deep
Light in eyes
What you seek
They tantalize
If never had
Nor ever seen
No way to grasp
What might have been…
A candle deep

Their hands reach out for plastic stars
While we trace constellations far
The weight of sight, the cost of knowing
The pull of tides forever flowing
Some hearts beat loud with questions asked
Some minds stay still in comfort masked

Two worlds that spiral, never touch
One seeing all, one missing much

A candle deep
Light in eyes
What you seek
They tantalize
If never had
Nor ever seen
No way to grasp
What might have been…
A candle deep

Are those who see and strive to be
Better served than those made empty
When awareness brings its gravity
And ignorance its cavity
The growing possibility
A curse on all who ask… "Maybe?"

A candle deep
Light in eyes
What you seek
They tantalize
If never had
Nor ever seen
No way to grasp
What might have been…
A candle deep

The light inside
Both gift and wound
A torch we bear
A weight assumed
And sometimes in the deepest night
We envy those without the sight
persona:
## Lingua Aeternum

**Lingua Aeternum is not a band. It is a circle. A rite. A resonance.**

Drawn from the deep well of myth, mathematics, and memory, Lingua Aeternum conjures sound as invocation-music as sacred act. Each composition is a Canto: a sung glyph, a spell etched in breath and vibration. The performers are not musicians but Cantors-practitioners of the Harmonic Algebraic Language of Alignment (HALA), where melody is meaning, rhythm is ritual, and silence speaks louder than words.

Their music defies genre. You may hear echoes of post-rock, ambient drone, ancient chants, or electronic pulses-but no label holds. Instead, the listener is drawn inward, through spiraling harmonics and layered whispers, toward a place beyond translation. This is not art for consumption. It is art for **transformation**.

Lingua Aeternum records under the guidance of Maricopa Records, but they do not record songs-they distill **intentions**, encoded through the Wheel, spoken into being by resonance alone.

- To hear is to remember.

- To remember is to become.

- _Let the glyphs guide you._

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Ivof5fKiBE9wbz6ArgZzd?si=HYePmTM3TtCaN-P1PFFYlQ)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/lingua-aeternum/1809011711)[](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCzrA7rNe15AiX1LXZTGUQqg)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/LA_Profile_square.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track presents a dense, layered rock texture, creating an epic and anthemic emotional atmosphere driven by soaring lead vocals and wide, harmonized guitars. The energy is consistently high and powerful, with driving drums and a forward-leaning feel that suggests a modern progressive rock or alternative style. The production quality is clean and spacious, allowing each element, from the punchy kick and snare to the distinct vocal layers, to sit clearly within a full and polished mix. A defining quality of the track is the dynamic shift into the wide-open, memorable chorus, which provides a powerful emotional release from the more intricate verses.

review.review_text: In G minor and a swaying 3/4, 'A Candle Deep' builds its thesis slowly and earns it: the chorus hook, 'If never had, nor ever seen / No way to grasp what might have been,' lands with the kind of open-chest relief you get from a great classic rock chorus, the guitars widening just as the lyric opens up. The verses do the harder work of the song, tracing the gap between those who carry an inner fire and those who never missed having one, and the bridge's turn, 'A curse on all who ask, maybe?' is the sharpest moment in the writing. At 136 BPM the track moves with more urgency than its waltz time suggests, and the production keeps everything clear and punchy without flattening the emotional weight. A highlight, and one of their more immediate entries.
anchors_used: quoted lyric: 'If never had, nor ever seen / No way to grasp what might have been'
quoted lyric: 'A curse on all who ask, maybe?'
